skill.name: seo-audit
skill.description: Audit websites for technical SEO, on-page optimization, content quality, indexation, and authority issues.
skill.summary: Find practical SEO issues and prioritize fixes that improve crawlability, indexation, rankings, and search visibility.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography and reconnaissance patterns, but the matched lines are ordinary SEO checklist language and tool names. No executable code, network calls, credential handling, prompt injection, or data exfiltration behavior was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The static matches are SEO words such as description, descriptive, desktop, and duplicate. These lines do not define or invoke DES, MD5, SHA-1, or any cryptographic operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines are human-readable SEO guidance, not code or configuration. No cryptographic API, algorithm choice, hashing, or encryption behavior is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: The matched lines mention SSL certificate review and paid SEO tools. They describe normal SEO audit checks and do not collect host data, enumerate files, or run local commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is a written checklist for SEO review. There is no instruction to execute system probes or access local system information.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: The matched lines are headings or SEO business-context checks, including mobile friendliness, trust signals, and local business guidance. They do not instruct scanning ports, probing networks, or contacting unauthorized targets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The terms appear inside non-executable SEO audit instructions. No network scanner, endpoint enumeration, or unauthorized discovery workflow is described.

content.user_title: Audit SEO Issues Across Website Pages
content.value_statement: SEO problems can hide in crawlability, indexation, page structure, and content quality. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through a practical audit with prioritized fixes.
actual_capabilities: Reviews crawlability, robots.txt, XML sitemaps, canonical tags, and indexation signals.
Checks Core Web Vitals, mobile readiness, HTTPS coverage, and URL structure.
Evaluates title tags, meta descriptions, headings, content depth, and keyword targeting.
Assesses internal linking, image optimization, duplicate content, and thin content risks.
Organizes findings by impact, evidence, fix, and priority for action planning.
Adapts common issue checks for SaaS, e-commerce, blog, and local business sites.
limitations: It does not crawl a live website unless the host AI tool has separate browsing or crawling access.
It cannot verify private Search Console or analytics data without user-provided access or exports.
It provides SEO recommendations, not guaranteed ranking outcomes.
It does not implement fixes directly unless used with tools that can edit the target site.

use_cases.title: Diagnose Ranking Drops
use_cases.description: Review recent site changes, indexation signals, and on-page issues to identify likely causes of lost organic traffic.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Technical SEO Backlog
use_cases.description: Convert crawl, performance, and page-structure issues into prioritized tasks for engineering and content teams.

use_cases.title: Improve Key Landing Pages
use_cases.description: Audit titles, headings, metadata, intent fit, internal links, and content depth for high-value pages.

prompt_templates.title: Basic SEO Health Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner audit for one website or page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this page for SEO issues. Focus on crawlability, title tag, meta description, headings, content quality, internal links, and quick wins.

prompt_templates.title: Technical SEO Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate audit using provided crawl notes or page details.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these technical SEO details and identify crawlability, indexation, canonical, sitemap, speed, mobile, HTTPS, and URL structure issues. Prioritize each fix.

prompt_templates.title: Content and On-Page Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review for pages targeting specific keywords.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit these pages against their target keywords. Evaluate search intent, title tags, meta descriptions, headings, content depth, image alt text, and internal links.

prompt_templates.title: Full SEO Action Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced audit that combines technical, content, and business context.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a full SEO audit report for this site. Include executive summary, technical findings, on-page findings, content quality issues, evidence, impact, fixes, and a prioritized 30-day action plan.

output_examples.input: Audit a SaaS pricing page that has low organic traffic.
output: Executive summary with the top ranking barriers.
Findings for title tag, heading alignment, content depth, internal links, and schema opportunities.
Prioritized fixes grouped by quick wins and larger content improvements.

output_examples.input: Review crawl notes for a large e-commerce category section.
output: Technical findings for faceted navigation, duplicate URLs, canonical tags, and indexable category pages.
Impact ratings for crawl budget and duplicate content risks.
Action plan for canonical rules, sitemap cleanup, and category content improvements.

output_examples.input: Create an SEO backlog after a site migration.
output: Migration checks for redirects, HTTPS consistency, canonical tags, sitemap updates, and indexation changes.
Evidence needed from Search Console, analytics, and crawl data.
A priority list for engineering, content, and analytics follow-up.

best_practices: Provide page URLs, target keywords, crawl exports, Search Console notes, and recent site changes when available.
Ask for prioritized fixes with evidence, impact, owner, and expected effort.
Review the most valuable pages first before expanding to the full site.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for ranking guarantees or exact traffic forecasts from an audit.
Do not treat generic checklist output as final without page-specific evidence.
Do not ignore analytics, Search Console, or crawl data when those sources are available.

faq.question: Can this skill audit a live website?
faq.answer: It can guide a live-site audit when the AI tool has browsing, crawling, or user-provided page data.

faq.question: Does it replace a full SEO crawler?
faq.answer: No. It helps interpret findings and structure recommendations, but crawler data is still useful for large sites.

faq.question: Can it review Google Search Console exports?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide exported data or summaries, and it can connect coverage, query, and performance issues to recommendations.

faq.question: Is it useful for non-technical users?
faq.answer: Yes. It asks for business context and explains SEO issues with impact, evidence, and practical fixes.

faq.question: Can it help with e-commerce SEO?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes checks for thin category pages, duplicate product descriptions, faceted navigation, and out-of-stock handling.

faq.question: Will it write implementation code?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on audit guidance. Code changes require a capable tool and access to the site repository.
